page: Amazon Web Services r5b.xlarge is a memory-optimized fifth-generation instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor operating at 3.1 GHz. The configuration provides 4 dedicated vCPUs derived from 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. There is no local storage bundled with this instance, and the baseline network bandwidth is 1 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate that the r5b.xlarge exhibits modest compute and memory throughput capabilities. In stress-ng tests, both single-core and multi-core CPU performance fall into the bottom 25% tier. Similarly, memory bandwidth tests for both small cached blocks (187.97 GB/sec) and large uncached blocks (26.41 GB/sec read, 22.94 GB/sec write) are positioned in the bottom 25%. However, Geekbench single-core performance and PassMark memory scores achieve average ratings within the middle 50% tier. Multi-threaded tasks, such as bzip3 decompression and software compilation, remain in the lower performance percentiles.

The primary tradeoff of the r5b.xlarge is its high memory capacity relative to its modest processing power and baseline network throughput. While it offers a cost-efficient entry point for memory-intensive workloads that do not require high CPU performance, it is constrained by a 1 Gbps baseline network speed and the lack of local NVMe storage. This profile is suitable for memory-bound applications, small-scale caching layers, lightweight database hosting, and memory-intensive enterprise applications that require a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio without demanding heavy parallel computation.
description: Amazon Web Services r5b.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs and 32.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, running on an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor at 3.1 GHz. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, this x86_64 server offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core but lacks local storage and is limited to a 1 Gbps baseline network speed. Benchmark data shows average Geekbench single-core and PassMark memory performance, though overall compute and memory bandwidth results generally fall into the bottom 25% tier. This instance provides a cost-effective option for memory-intensive workloads with moderate processing demands, such as small databases, caching layers, and memory-bound enterprise applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r5b.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 4 vCPUs, 32 GB DDR4 RAM, and an Intel Xeon 3.1 GHz CPU, designed for memory-bound workloads with moderate compute needs.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio for memory-bound workloads with moderate processing requirements.
